steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is the value of $f(x)$ when $x=0$. Since $x$ represents the number of candles made, $x=0$ is the moment before any candles are made. Evaluate: $f(0)=-9(0)+54=54$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Eli used approximately 9 ounces of wax for each candle.': 9 is the slope (rate of change per candle), not the $y$-intercept. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Eli had approximately 9 ounces of wax when he began to make the candles.': again confuses 9 (the slope) with the $y$-intercept. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Eli had approximately 54 ounces of wax when he began to make the candles.': $f(0)=54$ and $x=0$ means before any candles were made, so this is the starting amount of wax. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Eli used approximately 54 ounces of wax for each candle.': 54 is the $y$-intercept (a fixed starting value), not a per-candle rate. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
